mapped.summary: Mapped ground truth for ACE-Step 1.5 (repo at /Users/zathras/WAVESMITH_Project/engine/ACE-Step-1.5, git tag v0.1.8 confirmed via `git describe --tags`). Read README.md, docs/index.md, all 18 files under docs/en/, and skimmed docs/sidestep/ (training-only; no REST/macOS-inference content). Then corroborated every client-relevant claim against source. Headline findings for the TypeScript client: (1) The REST API surface in docs/en/API.md is accurate but incomplete — source adds /v1/create_sample, /v1/model_inventory, /v1/reinitialize, /v1/lora/*, hidden analysis-only flags, richer /health and /v1/models payloads, and extra per-request params (cover_noise_strength, repaint_mode/strength, track_name/track_classes). (2) Async flow is POST /release_task → poll POST /query_result (status 0/1/2; `result` is a JSON-encoded string) → GET /v1/audio?path=... (server only serves files under its own temp dir, 403 otherwise). (3) REST defaults differ from Python-API defaults: thinking=false, vocal_language="en", lm_cfg_scale=2.5, audio_format="mp3", batch_size→2. (4) LM is hard-skipped in source for cover/cover-nofsq/repaint/extract regardless of thinking. (5) macOS path: start_api_server_macos.sh exports ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND=mlx, port 8001, prefers embedded python_embedded/bin/python3.11 then uv; gpu_config.py applies MPS overrides (mlx backend, pt_mlx_only restriction, no offload/quant/compile). (6) All performance numbers (<2s A100, <10s 3090, tier speed table) are DOC-ONLY; there are NO Apple Silicon generation-time numbers anywhere in the docs — must benchmark locally. (7) Capability matrix: turbo/sft = text2music+cover+repaint(+cover-nofsq); extract/lego/complete need base (constants.py + UI gating); note the API's supported_task_types only distinguishes is_turbo, so SFT models report the base task list via /v1/models — a doc/source discrepancy. (8) 50 language codes + 'unknown', duration 10–600s (tier-clamped), batch max 8 (tier-clamped), BPM 30–300. (9) LRC and quality scoring exist only in the Gradio UI layer, not the REST API. (10) No explicit-content/safety filter exists anywhere in engine source; README has only a responsible-use disclaimer — moderation is the app's responsibility.

claims.topic: (a) REST API — SOURCE-CONFIRMED: thinking semantics and LM auto-skip
claims.claim: thinking=true makes the 5Hz LM generate audio codes (lm-dit); thinking=false runs pure DiT text2music. The LM (thinking AND all CoT) is hard-skipped in source for task types cover, cover-nofsq, repaint, and extract — `skip_lm_tasks = {"cover", "cover-nofsq", "repaint", "extract"}` — so LM only runs for text2music, lego, complete. Independent of thinking, missing metadata (bpm/keyscale/timesignature/duration) can be filled via LM CoT when use_cot_* flags are on; user-provided values win. Note API.md omits cover-nofsq from its task_type list but source accepts it.

claims.topic: (b) macOS / Apple Silicon — SOURCE-CONFIRMED: MPS runtime overrides
claims.claim: On macOS+MPS, gpu_config.get_gpu_config() overrides the VRAM tier defaults regardless of memory size: lm_backend_restriction="pt_mlx_only" (vllm requires CUDA), recommended_backend="mlx", offload_to_cpu_default=False and offload_dit_to_cpu_default=False (unified memory), quantization_default=False (torchao INT8 unsupported on MPS), compile_model_default=False, and MLX VAE decode chunk size auto-tuned by unified-memory size (256 ≤16GB, 512 ≤36GB, 1024 ≤64GB, 2048 above). Unified memory size is read via torch.mps recommended_max_memory; tier (and thus duration/batch caps) is then derived from that size. MLX path also covers VAE (MLXAutoEncoderOobleck rebuilt from the loaded PyTorch VAE) and the DCW sampler correction.

claims.topic: (e) Simple vs Custom mode — SOURCE-CONFIRMED mechanics
claims.claim: Gradio Generation Mode radio: Turbo/SFT models offer Simple, Custom, Remix, Repaint; pure Base adds Extract, Lego, Complete. Mode→task_type map: Simple/Custom→text2music, Remix→cover, Repaint→repaint, Extract→extract, Lego→lego, Complete→complete. Simple Mode = LM create_sample(query, instrumental, vocal_language) producing caption+lyrics+metas which then feed normal generation; over REST this is either (i) /release_task with sample_mode=true + sample_query, (ii) the undocumented POST /v1/create_sample {query, instrumental, vocal_language, temperature} which lazy-inits the LLM and returns {caption, lyrics, bpm, keyscale, duration, timesignature, vocal_language}, or (iii) /create_random_sample which does NOT use the LM — it returns a random canned example JSON from examples/simple_mode or examples/text2music. Custom mode enhancement = format_sample via use_format=true on /release_task or POST /format_input {prompt, lyrics, temperature, param_obj}. In Simple/sample mode the server sets use_cot_metas=false (metas already produced).

missed: CRITICAL for the TS client: GET /v1/models on the standalone API server (port 8001) is SHADOWED. route_setup.py:70-71 includes the OpenRouter-compatible router BEFORE register_model_service_routes (line 73); FastAPI matches first-registered routes, so GET /v1/models returns the OpenAI-style {"object":"list","data":[ModelInfo...]} (acestep/openrouter_adapter.py:594-651, acestep/openrouter_models.py:233-236) listing ONLY already-initialized models — not the API.md-documented {models, default_model, lm_models,...} payload. Use GET /v1/model_inventory instead (model_service_routes.py:185-189, explicitly commented 'non-OpenRouter internal endpoint').

POST /v1/chat/completions (OpenAI/OpenRouter-compatible, sync + SSE streaming with base64 audio) is mounted on the main API server at port 8001, not just acestep-openrouter:8002 (acestep/api/route_setup.py:70-71; acestep/openrouter_adapter.py:653). It performs NO authentication — even when ACESTEP_API_KEY is set, this endpoint (and the shadowed GET /v1/models) is open; do not assume the API key protects the whole surface.

Lazy model loading is the DEFAULT, not opt-in: startup_model_init.py:185 `no_init = env_bool("ACESTEP_NO_INIT", True)` — with no env vars set, models are NOT loaded at startup; the first /release_task triggers download+load (potentially minutes). Set ACESTEP_NO_INIT=false for eager loading; the --no-init CLI flag can only force true (server_cli.py:85-87). /health's models_initialized will be false until then.

The documented `timesteps` request parameter is dead on /release_task: it exists on GenerateMusicRequest (release_task_models.py:96-99) and in API.md:213, but build_generate_music_request (release_task_request_builder.py:39-104) never reads it from the parsed request, so client-supplied timesteps are silently ignored (job_generation_setup.py:147 always sees None). Likewise `lm_repetition_penalty` is parsed (builder:92) but never forwarded to GenerationParams or any LM call in the generation path.

CORS on the API server is hard-locked to localhost origins: allow_origins ["null","http://localhost","http://127.0.0.1"] + regex ^https?://(localhost|127\.0\.0\.1)(:\d+)?$, methods GET/POST/OPTIONS (route_setup.py:62-68). Browser fetches from any other origin fail — the Next.js app should call the engine server-side (API route/proxy), which is fine for the planned local setup but matters if the app is ever served from another host.

Progress reporting is fine-grained, answering the analyst's own open question: a progress callback flushes to the job store and local cache whenever progress advances ≥1%, the stage changes, or 0.5s elapses (job_blocking_generation.py:132-159), and running /query_result items include numeric `progress` (0..1) and `stage` (local_cache_updates.py:148-156) plus `progress_text` from the global log buffer (query_result_service.py:59) — sufficient for a real progress bar, not just queued/running/succeeded.

No server-side batch_size cap exists in the REST path, and the VRAM auto-reduce guard is a no-op on Apple Silicon: _vram_guard_reduce_batch returns the batch unchanged when device is 'mps' or 'cpu' (acestep/core/generation/handler/memory_utils.py:148-150). The WAVESMITH client should clamp batch_size itself (tier tables suggest ≤8; API.md's 'max 8' is not enforced).

/v1/create_sample and /format_input lazy-init the LLM using env defaults with backend fallback 'vllm' (sample_format_routes.py:78-81, 200-203): if the server is launched without start_api_server_macos.sh (which exports ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND=mlx), these endpoints will attempt vllm on macOS. Per-request lm_backend/lm_model_path on /release_task do feed lazy LLM init (llm_readiness.py:61-64), but /v1/create_sample and /format_input read env only.

If ACESTEP_INIT_LLM resolves to disabled, LLM-dependent endpoints (/format_input, /v1/create_sample) return HTTP 503 'LLM not initialized. Set ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=true in .env to enable.' (sample_format_routes.py:70-74, 192-196) — the client needs a distinct handling path for 503 vs 500 here. ACESTEP_INIT_LLM supports auto/true/false with GPU auto-detection (startup_llm_init.py:29-48).

Two additional env knobs not in the analyst's list: ACESTEP_API_WORKERS sets the ThreadPoolExecutor size for blocking generation (lifespan_runtime.py:117-118, default 1 — keep at 1 per GPU), and ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_CHUNK overrides the MLX VAE decode chunk heuristic (gpu_config.py:794-799, min 192). Also ACESTEP_LM_DEVICE / ACESTEP_LM_OFFLOAD_TO_CPU are consulted at LLM init (sample_format_routes.py:90-91).

On HTTP 429 (queue full), uploaded multipart temp files are deleted immediately before the error is raised (release_task_route.py:68-74), and per-job temp uploads are deleted after job completion via app.state.job_temp_files cleanup — re-submission after 429 must re-upload files.

The /release_task JSON path also accepts bodies with wrong/missing Content-Type via a raw-body JSON sniff (release_task_request_parser.py:159-181) and urlencoded fallback (182-197); unsupported types get HTTP 415 with a hint (199-205). Malformed JSON → 400 'Malformed JSON payload'. Useful for robust client error mapping.

MPS unified-memory detection fallback chain matters for tier selection on the target Mac: torch.mps.recommended_max_memory() → get_device_properties → sysctl hw.memsize × 0.75 → 8GB conservative default (gpu_config.py:589-628). MAX_MPS_VRAM env can simulate other tiers for testing (gpu_config.py:484-485).

store-fallback /query_result results (when the local cache misses) return a slimmer success item without generation_info/seed_value/lm_model/dit_model (query_result_service.py:84-117) — the TS client's result parser should treat those fields as optional.

The default DiT instruction and per-task instruction templates are resolved server-side, and lego/extract instructions embed {TRACK_NAME}: omitting track_name on lego/extract falls back to a generic '<task>_default' instruction rather than erroring (job_generation_setup.py:42-67; constants.py:128-139) — relevant if WAVESMITH ever exposes stem-oriented tasks.
